detail: New Kingdom Egypt — temples, hieroglyphs, and Nile trade.
Phoenician traders carry the alphabet across the Mediterranean.
The Olmec — mother culture of Mesoamerica.
Classical Athens — philosophy, democracy, and theatre.
Mauryan India — Ashoka, Buddhism, and statecraft.
Hellenistic Alexandria — the Great Library.
Han China — the Silk Road opens; paper is invented.
Imperial Rome — law, roads, the silver denarius.
Teotihuacan — one of the largest cities on Earth.
Aksum (Ethiopia) — Red Sea trade and its own coinage.
Gupta India — Nalanda university, the zero, astronomy.
Tang China — cosmopolitan heart of the Silk Road.
Abbasid Baghdad — the House of Wisdom and the gold dinar.
Umayyad Córdoba — Europe's most learned city.
Heian Japan — courtly arts and The Tale of Genji.
Classic Maya — astronomy and the Long Count calendar.
Tiwanaku — Andean highland civilization.
Samarkand — Silk Road crossroads of trade and learning.
Khmer Angkor — vast temple-city and hydraulic engineering.
Song China — printing, the compass, the first paper money.
Fatimid Cairo — al-Azhar and Mediterranean trade.
Great Zimbabwe — gold traded to the Indian Ocean.
Venice — Mediterranean commerce and the gold ducat.
Mali & Timbuktu — Mansa Musa's gold, Sankoré's scholars.
Aztec Tenochtitlan — a lake-city of 200,000.
Vijayanagara — South India's great empire.
Inca Cusco — the largest empire of the Americas.
Renaissance Florence — the Medici bank and the florin.
Ming China — the Forbidden City and Zheng He's fleets.
Ottoman Istanbul — the bridge of Europe and Asia.
Portugal — the Age of Discovery and the spice routes.
Spanish silver — the 'piece of eight', the first global currency.
Dutch Golden Age — the VOC, the first stock exchange, the guilder.
Edo Japan — a city of a million in two centuries of peace.
The Enlightenment — salons and the Encyclopédie.
Britain — the Industrial Revolution and the pound sterling as world reserve.
The American century — Wall Street and the US dollar.
Silicon Valley — the information age.
